title: Kagami — Content Moderation Trace
description: Per-message moderation trace analytics. Answers questions that the aggregate counter dashboards can't: which specific messages missed skip opportunities, where classifier and remote disagreed, which bad_hint anchors are firing, full audit trail for any non-NONE action. Requires content_moderation.trace.enabled=true and a Loki push sink.

panels.title: Trace stream rate (req/s, last 5m)
panels.description: Per-message traces emitted per second. Equals the total ContentModerator::check() rate when tracing is enabled. Zero means traces are not being emitted — check content_moderation.trace.enabled in kagami.json.

panels.title: Missed skip opportunities (remote ran, action=NONE)
panels.description: Messages where the remote classifier fired AND the final action was NONE — i.e. we paid for an OpenAI call on a clean message that could have been skipped by a Layer 2 shortcut. Each row is a wasted API spend candidate. If this list is large, consider: raising confidence_low_clean, enlarging the safe_hint anchor list, or enabling trust_bank.

targets.expr: {job="kagami_mod_trace"} | json | layers_remote_ran="true" | decision_final_action="NONE" | line_format "[{{.ipid}}] conf={{.layers_local_classifier_max_confidence}} sh={{.layers_safe_hint_max_similarity}} msg={{.message}}"

panels.title: Classifier vs remote disagreement (classifier hot, remote cold)
panels.description: Messages where the local classifier scored max_confidence > 0.5 but the remote classifier came back with toxicity < 0.1. These are potential false positives in the local model — classifier is flagging benign content as borderline-toxic. Use this feed for weekly calibration review: if the pattern repeats, consider retraining or raising confidence_high_skip.

targets.expr: {job="kagami_mod_trace"} | json | layers_local_classifier_max_confidence > 0.5 | layers_remote_ran="true" | layers_remote_scores_toxicity < 0.1 | line_format "lc_conf={{.layers_local_classifier_max_confidence}} lc_axis={{.layers_local_classifier_max_category_index}} remote_tox={{.layers_remote_scores_toxicity}} msg={{.message}}"

panels.title: Bad-hint anchor hit rankings (anchor curation feedback)
panels.description: Messages that matched a bad_hint anchor, sorted descending by similarity. Use this to review whether the anchor list is drifting: if matches are consistently at similarity > 0.85 with obvious hate patterns, the anchors are well-tuned. If matches are hovering at 0.75-0.78 on mundane content, the threshold is too loose or an anchor needs pruning. Review weekly; add/remove anchors based on observed false-positive rate.

panels.title: Enforcement audit (every non-NONE action)
panels.description: Every moderation decision with action ≠ NONE — log, censor, drop, mute, kick, ban. Full trace context for human review. When a user appeals a mute, this is the source of truth: which layer fired, what the scores were, which axis triggered, and the message text that caused it. Retain as long as your privacy policy allows; this IS the enforcement audit trail.

targets.expr: {job="kagami_mod_trace"} | json | decision_final_action!="NONE" | line_format "[{{.decision_final_action}}] [{{.ipid}}] skip={{.decision_skip_reason}} reason={{.decision_reason}} msg={{.message}}"

panels.title: Classifier max_conf p50 / p90 on clean traffic
panels.description: Rolling p50 and p90 of the local classifier's max_confidence output, filtered to messages where the final action was NONE. This is the metric that tells you whether confidence_low_clean is set correctly: if p90 is below your threshold, most clean traffic is correctly skipping the remote; if p90 is above, you're escalating too many benign messages and should raise the threshold. Calibrate by reading this line for a week on real traffic.

panels.title: Layer 2 skip reason distribution
panels.description: Rate of each Layer 2 skip reason over time, derived from the trace stream. Mirrors the kagami_moderation_layer2_skipped_total counter but sourced from traces so you can cross-reference against the per-message panels above. A healthy API-cost-optimized deployment has bad_hint/local_classifier_bad + trust_bank + safe_hint/local_classifier_clean collectively dominating the remote-call rate.
